key_insight: Peak mirror stresses from multi-mode M1M3 bending corrections can be estimated rapidly by root-sum-square (RSS) combining precomputed unit-mode stress fields, not just displacements.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-instant stress-margin checks and actuator-force optimization for the Vera C. Rubin Observatory’s active optics system during telescope operations.
method: Compute unit displacement and unit principal-stress fields for the first 20 NASTRAN bending modes.
Combine selected mode amplitudes with RSS to predict peak major principal stress.
Validate RSS stress estimates against full NASTRAN multi-mode simulations for representative corrections.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that RSS-based peak major principal stress predictions agree with NASTRAN results to within a few percent across the mirror substrate.
watch_item: The RSS approach is an approximation and should be revalidated for mode combinations or operating conditions that strongly change stress localization or nonlinear behavior.
paper_title: Determining stress-based bending mode limits for the Vera C. Rubin Observatory M1M3 active mirror system
